title: Consumer-Return Latency as the Control Parameter Between K-Shaped Divergence and Cyclic Growth
description: A dynamical-systems framing of distributional macro-stability: the latency of the consumer-return loop, not its level, is the control parameter selecting K-shaped divergence vs. a bounded rising cycle. Includes a minimal model with a delay-induced Hopf bifurcation, a curve-fitting classifier, real-data estimates (US 1967-2018, 1989-2026, and a COVID natural experiment), and a velocity-first consumer-equity instrument design rule. Text CC-BY-4.0; code MIT.
